tweets.text:
Horrible news coming out about Alpha Futures. 

Unfortunately this is all too common for the prop industry. 

It’s also exactly why we built Breakout the way we did. 

Flexing huge payouts means nothing if they can be denied. 

Trust is paramount in this industry. 

We're backed by Kraken and are the safest option in a sketchy industry. 

Don’t fall prey to counterparty risk.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Another major prop firm just rugged its own traders.

Approved payouts, gone. Funded accounts, closed. Called it a refund.

Sad part is this isn't surprising anymore.
It's becoming the norm.

That's why Breakout is built different.
Backed by @krakenfx, a tier one exchange.

thread_tweets.text:
We didn’t rush to the bottom on pricing, no massive discounts, we have really good abuse detection (cross-account hedging, fraud), and our plans have tighter drawdowns which allow for no shitty rules. 

Then we just stare at the data and adjust as necessary (eg we no longer offer a 2-step, it was subset gracefully without nuking any accounts or payouts).
thread_tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: war on.

No visible inbound tankers in the last 24 hours.

Reports stated that Iran fired at two vessels today. There was an escort that saw tankers exit, ATARAKTOS, was one of the VLCCs that exited.

With no diplomatic resolution for the Oman lane, we will likely see more escalation throughout this week. 

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker list.

tweets.text:
Finally reading "Broken Money" by @LynAldenContact and it's a good reminder that $BTC and other internet-native hard monies will most reward the patient ($ZEC the most credible complement to $BTC, in this category). 

From 2016-2025 there was a wave of impatience as the promise of blockchains drew crowds of people that didn't deeply understand Bitcoin, and treated the space as simply another tech platform. Bitcoin is more of a social reboot, than a technological one, and such a reboot takes decades to play out. 

Now, impatience is washing out to other seemingly faster moving waters, taking us closer to what things felt like in 2015 and before... which will once again reward those that understood all along.
